channel.title: Google on The Gamer's Hollow
channel.description: Recent content in Google on The Gamer's Hollow
channel.copyright: ©2023 The Gamer's Hollow | Hero Image by Lorenzo Herrera on Unsplash

item.title: Running Windows on a Chromebook
item.description:
A guide to installing windows on a Lenove 100e gen2 ast chromebook (for archive purposes)

What you need

  1. A Lenovo 100e/300e gen2 ast Chromebook.
  2. A sd card or usb drive.
  3. A mouse (wired, or dongle only).
  4. Power adapter for the chromebook.
  5. Ventoy downloaded for your OS
  6. Drivers downloaded from this guide.
  7. Tiny11 iso downloaded from this guide.

Downloads

  1. Tiny 11 ISO
  2. Ventoy
  3. Visual C++ Redist
  4. CR50 TPM Driver
  5. Chrome EC Driver
  6. Radeon GPU Drivers !!DO NOT INSTALL UNTIL READING THE GUIDE!!
  7. Radeon GPU Driver Patch
  8. Touchpad Drivers
  9. Touchscreen Drivers
  10. Audio Drivers
  11. 7Zip
  12. Supermium

Getting Started

An Overview

This guide will show you how to go from chromeOS to Windows 11, you will need an internet connection for the chromebook, and also the other requirements listed above

item.title: Linus Media Group Got Hacked
item.description:
On March 23, 2023, the tech review giant - Linus Tech Tips’ main YouTube channel was hacked and turned into a crypto scam channel. Linus, the CEO of the company, was rudely awakened at 3 am to multiple reports of the hack. The LTT community rallied against the hackers, even going as far as using superchats to warn viewers of the scam livestream.

The hack was not complicated and many channels on YouTube have been hacked in the same way. ThioJoe’s YouTube channel (youtube.com/@thiojoe) explains how channels are hacked. Typically, hackers send fake emails, such as a sponsorship or a YouTube DMCA email, with a PDF or other attachment. When the attachment is opened, it grabs the browser’s session token for that Google account. For more information on this process, I recommend watching ThioJoe’s video .
